message:
fix(node): enforce failed-forward-step invariant in saga executor

The saga executor previously allowed forward step execution to continue after
a step had already failed, and always unconditionally incremented
completed_steps and set state to Running regardless of step outcome. This
violated the saga pattern's core invariant: once any forward step fails, the
saga must transition to Failed state and require compensation before any
further forward execution.

Changes to SagaExecutor::execute_step():
- Add pre-execution check: if any existing record shows a failed forward step,
  reject the execution request with an error and set state to Failed
- Post-execution: only increment completed_steps and set Running for successful
  outcomes; failed outcomes set state to Failed without incrementing

New tests:
- test_failed_step_sets_failed_state_and_does_not_increment_completed_steps:
  Verifies that a failed step transitions to Failed and leaves completed_steps
  unchanged at the pre-failure count
- test_cannot_execute_additional_forward_steps_after_failure: Verifies that
  subsequent execute_step calls after failure are rejected, and that
  compensation still works on the failed saga

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
